serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled lean beef sirloin steak is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one medium steak portion as commonly served in a Western meal context.

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo database (data_source: hpb, confidence 0.95) for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported by HPB are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef top sirloin steak, lean only, cooked, grilled (NDB No. 13463), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. Water content sourced from USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled lean beef sirloin steak contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI value is assigned per University of Sydney GI Database methodology for non-carbohydrate foods. The numeric value 0 is used here as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to indicate that GI testing is not relevant for this food.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (49 mg/100 g) and high potassium (350 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Grilled lean beef sirloin steak is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt or high-sodium marinades; its naturally low sodium and high potassium content support blood pressure management per DASH guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar eliminate any glycaemic impact; high protein (30.5 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean muscle maintenance without raising blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled lean beef sirloin steak is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains no carbohydrates and will not raise postprandial blood glucose; pair with non-starchy vegetables and avoid sugary sauces or glazes per ADA dietary recommendations.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (1.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol (70 mg/100 g) are present at moderate levels; the lean cut substantially reduces saturated fat compared to untrimmed sirloin, but red meat consumption frequency should be monitored.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean grilled sirloin steak may be consumed in moderate portions (up to 150 g, 2–3 times per week) by individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, provided total saturated fat intake remains within AHA guidelines of less than 6% of daily energy; avoid high-fat accompaniments and prioritise cooking without added fats.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat contains arachidonic acid, a precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids that can amplify RA inflammatory cytokine production; trace ruminant trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is also pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including lean beef sirloin and consider substituting with oily fish such as salmon or mackerel to obtain anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids consistent with EULAR dietary recommendations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled lean beef sirloin steak is a high-protein, low-fat red meat cut sourced from the rear sirloin portion of beef cattle. The steak is grilled until cooked through, developing a slightly charred exterior while retaining a juicy interior. All visible and separable internal and external fat is removed prior to and after cooking, yielding a lean muscle-only product. Per 100 g, it provides 158 kcal, 30.5 g protein, 3.8 g fat (of which 1.4 g is saturated), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 49 mg sodium, 350 mg potassium, 2.2 mg iron, and 70 mg cholesterol. The high protein content supports muscle synthesis and satiety. The absence of carbohydrates makes it glycaemically inert, suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. The low sodium and high potassium profile aligns with DASH dietary guidelines for blood pressure management. As a red meat, it contains arachidonic acid and trace ruminant trans fat, which may be pro-inflammatory for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. In Western culinary contexts, sirloin steak is a premium cut commonly served as a main course at dinner, often accompanied by vegetables, salad, or starch sides. It is classified as minimally processed and is suitable for halal dietary requirements when sourced from appropriately certified suppliers.

confidence_reason: Primary macronutrient values sourced directly from Singapore HPB Foodo database with confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrient values supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for the closest exact match (beef top sirloin steak, lean only, cooked, grilled). Both are approved primary sources. The food description is precise and the nutrient profile is internally consistent.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as grilled lean beef sirloin steak is a pure animal muscle protein with no carbohydrate-containing ingredients. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Confirmed zero per Singapore HPB Foodo database and USDA FoodData Central. Saturated fat (1.4 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (1.5 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (0.2 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB No. 13463 (beef top sirloin steak, lean only, cooked, grilled) as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB source and not reliably available for this specific lean grilled preparation in USDA SR Legacy at the required precision. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB source; limited published data for this specific lean grilled preparation confirmed via USDA FoodData Central. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB source; USDA SR Legacy reports negligible amounts for this cut but value is not confirmed at sufficient precision for this record. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value for non-carbohydrate foods; gi_category is set to not_applicable as this food contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and GI testing is not applicable per University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. Occasion tag 'breakfast' from input cultural data is not the primary meal context for this food; dinner and lunch are more typical. Halal suitability is conditional on sourcing from certified halal suppliers.

Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value with gi_category not_applicable, satisfying the validator requirement that glycemic_index be numeric while correctly indicating GI is not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate food.
